title: tests: fix rootless tests on Ubuntu

comments.body:
@giuseppe I'm struck by how these tests appear to fail with the "old" runc commit and the "new" one.  I am assuming the failure is caused by the same thing.  If so, that fact leads me to question if the failure ultimately is caused by some external dependency, like a container image used for the rootless tests or some automatic update of some kind (I didn't encode any).

What do you think, are the failures on master identical to what we see with the freshly built ``ubuntu-18-libpod-0c954a67`` image?

> What do you think, are the failures on master identical to what we see with the freshly built `ubuntu-18-libpod-0c954a67` image?

a broken runc. I've recently switched to the last runc available on Fedora: 87bf117417b9a27ed38a1a50d11e1e6dc2042c3f but apparently it is still broken

Let's try to get the newer version in Fedora:

https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2018-a6ae708d15
https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2018-504e197a65

I am surprised that tests passed for the PR that enabled the latest runc to be used

@cevich it looks like the runc in the image is not updated.  I am forcing an installation of runc now and the rootless tests are passing.  It is failing somewhere else now :(

```
Summarizing 3 Failures:
[Fail] Podman pause [It] podman remove a paused container by id with force 
/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/pause_test.go:133
[Fail] Podman pause [It] podman remove a paused container by id with force 
/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/pause_test.go:133
[Fail] Podman pause [It] podman remove a paused container by id with force 
/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/pause_test.go:133
Ran 475 of 503 Specs in 1809.846 seconds
FAIL! -- 474 Passed | 1 Failed | 0 Flaked | 0 Pending | 28 Skipped
--- FAIL: TestLibpod (1809.85s)
FAIL
```

ok so there is another patch in runc that might cause problems with `podman remove a paused container by id with force`.

I've forced pushed another version now of a safer commit in runc, this should be enough to pass the rootless tests.

`tests: always install runc on Ubuntu` is a hack until we sort out why runc is not updated in the image
